title: Heaps
description: Nini and Mimi are playing a game with $N$ heaps of stones and pebbles. Each heap $i$ has $B_i$ big stones and $S_i$ small pebbles. Nini and Mimi take turns performing moves and once a player has no more moves to do, they lose. Each move consists of choosing a non-empty heap 𝑖 and removing some stones and/or pebbles from it. Formally, one can remove $X$ stones and $Y$ pebbles, where $0 ≤ X ≤ B_i$, $0 ≤ Y ≤ S_i$ and $X + Y > 0$. However, every removed stone must be replaced with at least $K$ pebbles; it can be replaced with any natural number of pebbles not less than $K$. Thus, in any move where $X ≥ 1$, first $Y$ pebbles are removed and then the player must add back $Z ≥ KX$ pebbles, which are taken from an infinite supply of pebbles. Nini goes first. Before making her move she wonders whether she can win the game if she plays optimally. Write a program, which answers her question.
input: From the first line of the standard input, your program should read $K$ and $Q$. Then $Q$ independent tests with that $K$ will follow. For each test, the first line contains $N$. The next $N$ lines each have a desription of a heap: $B_i$ and $S_i$.
output: On $Q$ lines, your program should output the answers to each of the tests in the order they were given. It should print Win, if Nini can win, and Loss, otherwise.
source: Olympiad, International Autumn Tournament in Informatics, 2021, Group A (Seniors)
